metadata.rationale: The article is broadly grounded in a real viral post and a real long-running grievance by Sager, but it overstates the sense of a current Tim Cook-centered dispute and pairs cautious reporting with edited images that project a much stronger accusation than the text ultimately supports. The overall takeaway remains mostly accurate, yet the framing and visuals add non-trivial distortion.

prediction.taxonomy_rationale: The text overlays and colored boxes in the images are clear examples of visual editing that distort the meaning of the underlying document (claims of theft). The text presents a conspiracy narrative involving modern CEO Tim Cook and historical technical notes from the 2000s, creating a misleading context of an active corporate feud. This misalignment of timelines and entities constitutes contextual inconsistency.
